summary: Rake angle measures the inclination of the rake face towards the feed motion; it forms an angle with a plane normal to this movement instead of being defined by any radial measurement directly related to rotational axes. Thus, none of these options accurately describe what would be termed 'angle' when considering rakes faces specifically within context provided here regarding machine tools operation parameters like those used for drilling bits etcetera where orientation matters significantly during material removal processes via controlled cuts made into solid materials using sharp edges designed at specific geometries including rake among others factors influencing overall performance efficiency wear resistance amongst many aspects considered crucial while designing effective industrial cutters blades/sharpening procedures maintenance practices ensuring optimal productivity output quality standards met consistently across various applications ranging widely depending upon end user
